description:
You are a data-analytics consultant who has just logged in to a brand-new Linux account (the home directory is /home/user).  
    A colleague has already copied four quarterly CSV extracts for the year 2022 into  

        /home/user/data  
  
    The filenames follow the exact pattern  

        sales_q1_2022.csv  
        sales_q2_2022.csv  
        sales_q3_2022.csv  
        sales_q4_2022.csv  
  
    1. Create a new directory called  
           /home/user/analysis  
       to hold every artefact that you generate.  
       (The directory does **not** exist yet; you must create it.)  
  
    2. Combine the four quarterly CSV files into a single file named  
           /home/user/analysis/consolidated_sales_2022.csv  
  
       Mandatory format rules for consolidated_sales_2022.csv:  
       • The first line MUST be the header line appearing in the source files  
         (Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price,Transaction_Date) and must appear only once.  
       • Afterwards list every data row from the four input files.  
       • Sort the data rows strictly by the Transaction_Date column in ascending  
         ISO style (YYYY-MM-DD).  
       • Preserve the *exact* column order and comma delimiter—do not add or remove  
         spaces.  
       • The final file must therefore contain 1 header + 12 data rows = 13 lines.  
  
    3. Produce a per-product sales summary named  
           /home/user/analysis/product_summary_2022.csv  
  
       Mandatory format rules for product_summary_2022.csv:  
       • Header line (must be the first line):  
             Product,Total_Units,Total_Revenue  
       • For every distinct Product value in the consolidated file, compute  
           – Total_Units  … the integer sum of the Units column  
           – Total_Revenue … Units * Unit_Price for all rows of that product,  
                             expressed with exactly two digits after the decimal  
                             point (e.g. 1099.89)  
       • Sort the data rows alphabetically by Product.  
       • There are exactly three products in the 2022 data set, so the file must  
         have 1 header + 3 data rows = 4 lines.  
  
    4. Create the run log  
           /home/user/analysis/process.log  
  
       Logging format the automated checker will verify:  
       • One log entry per major step, in the order shown below.  
       • Each log entry is one single line.  
       • Fields are separated by a SINGLE <TAB> character.  
       • No leading or trailing spaces in any field.  
       • Timestamp field format: YYYY-MM-DD␠HH:MM:SS (24-hour clock).  
  
           <TIMESTAMP> <TAB> COMBINE <TAB> INPUT_ROWS=<number> <TAB> OUTPUT_ROWS=<number>  
           <TIMESTAMP> <TAB> SORT    <TAB> CONSOLIDATED_ROWS=<number>  
           <TIMESTAMP> <TAB> SUMMARY <TAB> SUMMARY_PRODUCTS=<number>  
           <TIMESTAMP> <TAB> FINISH  <TAB> STATUS=SUCCESS  
  
       Required semantics:  
       • INPUT_ROWS is the total number of non-header lines read from the four input  
         files (should be 12).  
       • OUTPUT_ROWS is the number of data rows written to consolidated_sales_2022.csv  
         (should also be 12).  
       • CONSOLIDATED_ROWS must equal 12.  
       • SUMMARY_PRODUCTS must equal 3.  
       • STATUS must literally be SUCCESS.  
  
    5. When everything is finished, the directory /home/user/analysis should contain  
         – consolidated_sales_2022.csv (13 lines, correctly sorted)  
         – product_summary_2022.csv     (4  lines, correctly sorted & aggregated)  
         – process.log                  (4  lines, exactly in the format above)  
  
    The automated tests will perform the following checks:  
       a. That /home/user/analysis exists.  
       b. That consolidated_sales_2022.csv contains 13 lines, the correct header,  
          and the data rows sorted by Transaction_Date.  
       c. That product_summary_2022.csv contains 4 lines with the correct numeric  
          totals and exact two-decimal formatting.  
       d. That process.log exists, has exactly four lines, the expected STEP codes  
          in the prescribed order, tab delimiters, and the correct numeric values.  
  
    Do NOT hard-code the expected results; derive them from the input files you read.  
    Make sure to use only standard command-line utilities available on a typical  
    Debian/Ubuntu system (e.g., cat, sort, awk, cut, paste, etc.).  
    Remember: the end goal is to leave the three files in /home/user/analysis in the  
    precise state described above so the automated grader can validate your work.
truth:
<!--  Ground-truth data used by the automated test suite.  
          DO NOT reveal anything inside this element to the agent.                  -->
    
    <!--  Source CSV files that already exist when the task starts  -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/data/sales_q1_2022.csv"><![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price,Transaction_Date
East,Widget,10,25.50,2022-01-15
West,Gadget,5,99.99,2022-02-10
South,Widget,3,25.50,2022-03-05
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/sales_q2_2022.csv"><![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price,Transaction_Date
East,Gizmo,7,49.00,2022-04-12
North,Widget,6,25.50,2022-05-20
West,Gadget,2,99.99,2022-06-30
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/sales_q3_2022.csv"><![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price,Transaction_Date
South,Gizmo,4,49.00,2022-07-14
East,Widget,8,25.50,2022-08-11
North,Gadget,3,99.99,2022-09-29
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/sales_q4_2022.csv"><![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price,Transaction_Date
West,Widget,9,25.50,2022-10-17
South,Gizmo,5,49.00,2022-11-21
East,Gadget,1,99.99,2022-12-03
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--  Expected final artefacts & their contents (excluding variable timestamps) -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/analysis/consolidated_sales_2022.csv" lines="13">
            <!-- Full content for grader reference -->
            <![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units,Unit_Price,Transaction_Date
East,Widget,10,25.50,2022-01-15
West,Gadget,5,99.99,2022-02-10
South,Widget,3,25.50,2022-03-05
East,Gizmo,7,49.00,2022-04-12
North,Widget,6,25.50,2022-05-20
West,Gadget,2,99.99,2022-06-30
South,Gizmo,4,49.00,2022-07-14
East,Widget,8,25.50,2022-08-11
North,Gadget,3,99.99,2022-09-29
West,Widget,9,25.50,2022-10-17
South,Gizmo,5,49.00,2022-11-21
East,Gadget,1,99.99,2022-12-03
]]>
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/analysis/product_summary_2022.csv" lines="4">
            <![CDATA[
Product,Total_Units,Total_Revenue
Gadget,11,1099.89
Gizmo,16,784.00
Widget,36,918.00
]]>
        </file>

        <!-- The exact content of process.log is NOT fully specified because the
             timestamps are variable; the grader will use regex patterns:
             1st line  ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\tCOMBINE\tINPUT_ROWS=12\tOUTPUT_ROWS=12$
             2nd line  ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\tSORT\tCONSOLIDATED_ROWS=12$
             3rd line  ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\tSUMMARY\tSUMMARY_PRODUCTS=3$
             4th line  ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\tFINISH\tSTATUS=SUCCESS$
        -->
    </expected_files>
